meaning: n.(欧洲国家的)宪兵队,(总称)宪兵
examples: Although the gendarmerie is ahead of the market the market is catching up.
Fred escorted his two shivering party members to a nearby inn while I and the remaining two literally ran to the gendarmerie post.
That convoy then dispersed to bases along the border, accompanied by police and gendarmerie teams for security, the agency reported.
The gendarmerie and the police were recently given a pay rise, but not the army.
The Belgian gendarmerie mounted their largest ever operation all for a game of football.
